hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The solver must trace checkpoint blobs, perception cache merge, modifier epochs, and planner inputs across Go and C boundaries.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Repair coordinates snapshot packing, cache restore, timed modifiers, native legality checks, and hostile planning.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose
hardness_axes.reasoning: The prompt reports illegal hostile steps after restore without naming cache invalidation or epoch rebasing.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Fixing visibility without epoch rebasing or planner inputs leaves other scenario rows failing.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: Requires cross-layer game-state debugging beyond a generic persistence tutorial.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The instruction can honestly state the audit outcome without revealing the internal coupling.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Multiple scripted profiles are checked, not a single secret corrupt artifact.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned fix requires coordinated code changes across several subsystem roots.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tests cover several profile combinations and require behavior that generalizes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A complete public contract still leaves the solver to diagnose why hostile steps drift.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty rests on system reasoning, not merely on anti-tamper measures.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The idea is semantic and not a verifier dependency or timeout task.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The flipping contract distributes failures so no small obvious cluster controls the suite.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle is planned as multi-file coordinated logic, not a whole-file replacement of one helper.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task cannot be solved by editing one table, manifest, or config block.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path symbols and planned test names avoid those nouns.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path function names are opaque and decoys structurally rhyme with them.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A standard visibility tutorial is insufficient because cache merge and epoch rebasing are tested.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task does not rely on security flavor for perceived hardness.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The requirements are coupled: visibility, epochs, and legality fixes affect each other.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The shell/Go/C harness provides realism but the claimed hardness is in the simulator semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model should need experiments and code tracing beyond opening obvious files.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The combined failure mode is clearly hard debugging rather than medium implementation work.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Four non-trivial discoveries are enumerated with planned locations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned instruction is symptoms-only and avoids cause disclosure.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three topologies each require at least three coordinated locations.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic harness output and move logs make results programmatically checkable.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The output path and legality contract can be stated concisely without overspecifying internals.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can repair the small Go/C system in a few hours.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: The task demands professional game-state and restore debugging.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Restore drift in turn-based games is operationally valuable to diagnose.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Tests grade move logs and legality rather than implementation choices.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md will not reveal how checkpoint blobs, observation cache, modifier epochs, and planner inputs interact.

discovery_budget.discovery: Checkpoint blobs omit part of the remembered hidden-tile set the uninterrupted path keeps.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming packing rules would make the repair site obvious.

discovery_budget.discovery: Restored runs merge stale observation entries without rebuilding from the live grid.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It would reduce the task to clearing one map.

discovery_budget.discovery: Timed modifiers use the wrong turn base after a checkpoint boundary.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It exposes epoch arithmetic that should be inferred from behavior.

discovery_budget.discovery: Hostile planners read observation data that disagrees with native wall checks.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It over-narrows the search to the planner boundary alone.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The planned prompt names illegal hostile behavior and the output path but does not identify causes, algorithms, files, flags, or thresholds.

attack_path.summary: Likely trivialization: hardcode the audit JSON or skip hostile turns after restore.
attack_path.reasoning: Planned tests independently recompute legality from layouts and config, so report-only patches cannot satisfy all six scenario rows.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Coordinate snapshot masks, cache invalidation, epoch rebasing, planner inputs, and C legality.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Any smaller patch should fail at least two of the clean, blind, dense, multi-hostile, epoch-boundary, or mixed scenarios.

collapse_audit.reasoning: The proposed construction leaves nontrivial residual work after the instruction and file tree are visible.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: The solver must synthesize runtime behavior across Go state, persisted blobs, native legality probes, and verifier-derived move logs.

renames_during_drafting.reason: Avoided fog vocabulary on the fix path.
Removed forbidden instruction nouns from the test surface.
